title: Limonium lobatum
taxonomy: Limonium lobatum is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Plumbaginaceae. This family is a group of plants commonly known as the leadworts or plumbago. The genus Limonium, to which Limonium lobatum belongs, comprises coastal plants, including sea lavender and statice. The classification of Limonium lobatum is further detailed with the binomial nomenclature of the species, indicating its precise place within the plant kingdom. Limonium lobatum is also known by other names, such as sea lavender, which can sometimes lead to confusion regarding its classification.

intro: Limonium lobatum, also known as sea lavender or statice, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bright yellow or purple flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Limonium lobatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, such as limonin and aucubin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Limonium lobatum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications for skin and wound care.
